They can be used to see what callbacks an XCMD/XFCN is making into HyperCard, and to change the results returned to the XCMD/XFCN. (Throughout this text XCMDs and XFCNs are referred to simply as XCMDs. XTrace and XTrap work with either). >>XTrace description XTrace loads and executes any XCMD and traps the callbacks the XCMD makes to HyperCard. It builds a list of the callbacks and their parameters, and puts the list into the HyperTalk global variable 'callbacks.' This list can be displayed to show what callbacks the XCMD made. The callbacks are executed, and the behavior of the XCMD is not affected. >>XTrap description XTrap is similar to XTrace, but rather than making a list of the callbacks, it sends a HyperTalk message to the current card with the callback information, and lets you write a handler to intercept the callback and even change the value returned. For example, suppose you use an XCMD that gets the name of the current stack and prints the stack name on a report, and you'd like it to print something else. You can trap the callback that asks for the stack name [EvalExpr('the short name of this stack')] and return something else. After using XTrace to see what callbacks an XCMD makes, you can use XTrap to change the results and modify the XCMD's behavior (or get more control over what the XCMD does to your stack).",
